description_long: This object is a vintage CRT computer monitor with a boxy, rectangular form and a slightly curved glass display at the front. The casing is primarily a light beige or off-white plastic, with visible wear and discoloration, especially on the rear and lower edges. The front features a thick bezel framing the screen, with horizontal ventilation slats and two small round buttons or indicator lights below the display. The rear of the monitor is solid, with subtle venting and a recessed area at the top, possibly for carrying or cable management. The monitor sits directly on its base, which is integrated into the main body, and there are no visible external stands or adjustable arms. No ports or brand logos are visible on any side of the monitor.
description: A vintage beige CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, curved glass screen, and integrated base, showing signs of wear and no visible logos or ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved glass screen, thick bezel, and two small round buttons or indicators below the display.
description_view_1: Front-side angle highlighting the monitor's depth, the sloped top with a recessed area, and the solid plastic casing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid back panel, subtle venting, and the recessed top area, with no visible ports or logos.
description_view_3: Rear-side angle emphasizing the monitor's bulk, the integrated base, and the worn, discolored plastic surface.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, made of matte beige plastic, with rounded corners and a slightly recessed frame around the display. It has a utilitarian, unadorned appearance with no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor has an integrated flat base that is part of the main body, with no visible separation or adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: Two small round buttons or indicators are located on the lower front bezel, beneath the screen, spaced apart horizontally.
